data.title: Coinbase: Optimistic about the performance of the crypto market in the second half of the year, beware of the systemic risks of listed companies buying BTC with high leverage
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Coinbase said in its latest report that it is optimistic about the trend of the crypto market in the second half of 2025, but at the same time warned that some listed companies' purchase of Bitcoin through convertible debt leverage may bring systemic risks.
The report pointed out that there are currently 228 listed companies holding a total of about 820,000 BTC, of ​​which about 20 adopt a financing model similar to MicroStrategy. If the market fluctuates sharply in the future or related debts mature in 2029-2030, passive selling pressure may be triggered. Coinbase said that short-term risks are controllable, and the key lies in the future refinancing ability of these companies. (TheBlock)

data.title: Key Economic Events to Watch Next Week
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, several significant economic events and data releases are scheduled for next week, all times are in UTC+8.

On Tuesday at 20:30, the United States will release the May retail sales monthly rate, with the previous value recorded at 0.10%.

On Wednesday at 20:30, the initial jobless claims for the week ending June 14 will be announced, with the prior figure at 248,000.

On Thursday at 02:00, the Federal Reserve will reveal its interest rate decision, with the previous upper limit set at 4.50%.

Also on Thursday at 02:00, the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) will publish its rate decision and summary of economic projections.

Following this, at 02:30, Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell will hold a press conference on monetary policy.

data.title: Hong Kong’s Stablecoin Regulations Set to Take Effect August 1
data.description:
Catenaa, Saturday, June 14, 2025- Hong Kong will implement its new stablecoin regulatory framework starting August 1, 2025, following the ordinance’s ratification on May 30, officials have announced. The law establishes a licensing regime for stablecoin issuers and related service providers operating in the city. Authorities expect the first licenses to be granted by year-end. […]
source: https://investors.catenaa.com/news/hong-kongs-stablecoin-regulations-set-to-take-effect-august-1

data.title: Bitcoin Mining Difficulty Decreases Slightly
data.description: According to PANews, data from Cloverpool indicates that Bitcoin mining difficulty has been adjusted at block height 901,152. The mining difficulty has decreased by 0.45%, bringing it down to 126.41 T.

data.title: LBANK EDGE has launched FAITH(FAITH)
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to official news, LBANK EDGE has launched FAITH (FAITH) at 19:40 (SGT) on June 14, and opened the FAITH/USDT trading pair. At present, the deposit service is open, and the withdrawal service will be open at 20:00 (SGT) on June 15.
It is reported that FAITH is a community-driven Meme token based on BNB Chain. LBANK EDGE provides its trading users with a "transaction compensation" benefit. If there is a loss, a maximum subsidy of 100 USDT can be obtained. The activity ends at 19:40 on June 16.
LBANK EDGE is a trading zone that integrates cutting-edge value discovery mechanisms and institutional-level liquidity. It aims to accurately discover high-potential assets while providing excellent liquidity support and a multi-level user protection system.

data.title: Circle Launches USDC on XRP Ledger to Boost Cross-Chain Liquidity
data.description:
Circle has officially introduced the USD Coin (USDC) on the XRP Ledger, a significant development aimed at enhancing cross-chain liquidity within the cryptocurrency ecosystem. This strategic move seeks to facilitate smoother transactions, improve accessibility, and increase the utility of USDC across different blockchains, thereby fostering a more interconnected financial landscape.
source: https://voiceofcrypto.online/ampstories/web-stories/circle-launches-usdc-on-xrp-ledger-to-boost-cross-chain-liquidity
